name: Fermi Inc.
business: Fermi Inc. is building a private power campus for AI-centric customers, developing and leasing large-scale, grid-independent energy generation and high-performance computing facilities for the hyperscale era. The company's strategy is anchored by Project Matador in the Texas Panhandle, a multi-phased development designed to deliver up to 11 GW of private power generation capacity and support up to approximately 15 million square feet of AI-ready hyperscale compute infrastructure. The company plans to generate revenue from long-term tenant lease arrangements, including fixed rent based on reserved power capacity and pass-through reimbursements, with power provided as an incident of tenancy.
sellsTo: hyperscale and other large-scale compute users
enterprises or governmental and quasi-governmental entities
namedSuppliers: Southwestern Public Service Company (SPS, a subsidiary of Xcel Energy Inc.)
CSG Investments (affiliate of Beal Bank USA)

exposures.sentence: Fermi Inc. (Fermi, we, us, or our) exists to power the artificial intelligence needs of tomorrow.

We are building a private power campus for AI-centric customers developing and leasing large-scale, grid-independent energy generation and high-performance computing facilities purpose-built for the hyperscale era.
Phase 3 contemplates the development and construction of nuclear baseload generation capacity at Project Matador, beginning with the initial 1 GW Westinghouse Reactor.
We expect BESS to play an important role in campus power quality and reliability.
We intend to incorporate solar generation and battery energy storage systems ("BESS") at Project Matador to provide multiple reliability, cost reduction, and environmental benefits.
We have secured up to 200 MW of grid-supplied capacity pursuant to our ESA with SPS.

Our ability to execute our strategy depends in part on attracting, developing, and retaining qualified personnel with specialized technical, regulatory, and project execution experience, and on maintaining a culture of accountability, safety, and disciplined execution.

catalysts.event: Final approval from TCEQ for approximately 6 GW natural gas-fired air permit
catalysts.sentence: On February 25, 2026, we received final approval from TCEQ for our approximately 6 GW Clean Air Permit, which we believe represents one of the largest natural gas-fired air permits issued in the Western Hemisphere.

catalysts.event: Filed additional application with TCEQ for incremental 5 GW air permit
catalysts.sentence: On March 27, 2026, we filed an additional application with the TCEQ for an incremental 5 GW Clean Air Permit.

catalysts.event: NRC accepted combined license application for four Westinghouse AP1000 reactors
catalysts.sentence: We filed a combined license application with the NRC for four Westinghouse AP1000 reactors, and the NRC accepted the application for review on September 5, 2025.

catalysts.event: Initial 200 MW grid-supplied capacity expected to be energized (first 86 MW in second half 2026, incremental 114 MW targeted by year-end 2026)
catalysts.sentence: The first 86 MW of this capacity is expected to be energized in the second half of 2026. We are working to accelerate energization of the remaining 114 MW by year-end 2026.

catalysts.event: Expected to achieve approximately 2.0 GW of gas-fired and grid-supplied power online by end of 2027
catalysts.sentence: Based on our secured equipment position and contracted procurement arrangements, we continue to expect to achieve our longer-term objective of reaching approximately 2.0 GW of gas-fired and grid-supplied power online by the end of 2027, subject to completion of installation, commissioning, securing project financing, and tenant readiness milestones.

tamClaims.claim: Total AI power demand is projected to grow from 55 GW in 2023 to up to 219 GW by 2030 according to McKinsey & Company.
tamClaims.sentence: Total AI power demand is projected to grow from 55 GW in 2023 to up to 219 GW by 2030 according to McKinsey & Company, with AI workloads forecasted to increase by a factor of 3.5 over the same period, necessitating an estimated $5.2 trillion in related infrastructure investment.

tamClaims.claim: The global generative AI market is expected to grow from $64 billion in 2023 to $457 billion by 2027 according to Bloomberg Intelligence.
tamClaims.sentence: The global generative AI market is expected to grow from $64 billion in 2023 to $457 billion by 2027 according to Bloomberg Intelligence.

tamClaims.claim: In 2024, capital expenditure on AI-focused data center development reached $210 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: In 2024, capital expenditure on AI-focused data center development reached $210 billion, with only $39 billion allocated to ongoing operating costs, indicating significant emphasis on upfront infrastructure buildout.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. data center demand by 2030 is expected to land between 1,000 terawatt-hours and over 1,534 terawatt-hours per year across a range of industry sources.
tamClaims.sentence: U.S. data center demand by 2030 is expected to land between 1,000 terawatt-hours and over 1,534 terawatt-hours per year across a range of industry sources.
